title: Cross-machine MQTT interoperability validation
description:
## Intent
Validate cross-machine realtime interoperability over MQTT so one machine can publish mutations and another machine's running console hub receives them, writes overlay updates, and pushes immediate SSE/UI updates.

## Test Topology
- Machine A: Broker host (Mosquitto)
- Machine B: Console hub + UI watcher (kbsc / kanbus-console)
- Machine C: Producer (kbs or kanbus mutating commands)

Minimum topology for smoke test:
- Machine A and Machine B can be the same host.
- Machine C must be a different host from Machine B for cross-machine validation.

## Broker Setup (Machine A)
1. Create LAN test config:
   - listener 1883 0.0.0.0
   - allow_anonymous true
   - persistence false
2. Start broker:
   - mosquitto -c ~/mosquitto-lan.conf -v
3. Record broker endpoint:
   - mqtt://<BROKER_IP>:1883

## Receiver Setup (Machine B)
1. Configure realtime for MQTT:
   - KANBUS_REALTIME_TRANSPORT=mqtt
   - KANBUS_REALTIME_BROKER=mqtt://<BROKER_IP>:1883
   - KANBUS_REALTIME_AUTOSTART=false
2. Start console hub:
   - kbsc (or kanbus-console)
3. Open UI route:
   - http://127.0.0.1:<PORT>/local/kanbus/
4. Optional SSE verification:
   - curl -N http://127.0.0.1:<PORT>/api/events/realtime

## Producer Setup (Machine C)
1. Configure matching MQTT endpoint:
   - KANBUS_REALTIME_TRANSPORT=mqtt
   - KANBUS_REALTIME_BROKER=mqtt://<BROKER_IP>:1883
   - KANBUS_REALTIME_AUTOSTART=false
2. Run mutation from kbs and kanbus:
   - kbs comment <ISSUE_ID> "MQTT cross-machine test <timestamp>"
   - kanbus comment <ISSUE_ID> "MQTT cross-machine test <timestamp>"

## Expected Results
- Console UI on Machine B updates immediately for each new comment.
- SSE stream emits issue_updated events with issue_data payload.
- Overlay snapshot/tombstone behavior remains consistent with existing merge rules.
- Mutation commands succeed even if publish fails (best-effort policy).

## Optional Wire-Level Validation
- Subscribe on broker host:
  - mosquitto_sub -h <BROKER_IP> -p 1883 -t 'projects/+/events' -v
- Confirm envelopes arrive for both Rust and Python producers.

## Troubleshooting Checklist
- Verify broker host firewall allows TCP/1883.
- Confirm all machines use identical broker endpoint and topic template.
- Confirm no stale local-only transport env vars are set.
- Confirm producer command output has no realtime publish warning.

## Acceptance Criteria (BDD)
Feature: Cross-machine MQTT interoperability
  Scenario: Rust producer updates remote console in realtime
    Given a reachable MQTT broker
    And a running console hub subscribed via MQTT on machine B
    When machine C runs `kbs comment <id> ...`
    Then machine B UI receives an immediate issue update
    And SSE emits issue_updated for that issue

  Scenario: Python producer updates remote console in realtime
    Given a reachable MQTT broker
    And a running console hub subscribed via MQTT on machine B
    When machine C runs `kanbus comment <id> ...`
    Then machine B UI receives an immediate issue update
    And SSE emits issue_updated for that issue
creator: ryan.porter
